Title: The Torreya grandis genome illuminates the origin and evolution of gymnosperm-specific sciadonic acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Torreya grandis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sciadonic acid biosynthesis; fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: cis-11,14-eicosadienoic acid (20:2Δ11,14), likely as phosphatidylcholine-linked intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates an 18-carbon Δ9 polyunsaturated fatty acid precursor to the 20-carbon intermediate cis-11,14-eicosadienoic acid in the sciadonic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Positive effects on human health, including reducing inflammation, lowering triglycerides, preventing blood clots and regulating lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of sciadonic acid by supplying its C20 precursor; co-expression with TgDES1 enables de novo sciadonic acid production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed kernels; expression increases during seed maturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In T. grandis seeds, expression increases during maturation; in transgenic Arabidopsis, ectopic overexpression in seeds via 35S construct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling during seed maturation; metabolite profiling by gas chromatography; subcellular colocalization with ER marker in N. benthamiana; heterologous overexpression with TgDES1 in Arabidopsis followed by GC detection of sciadonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgELO1 was identified as the seed-expressed candidate Δ9-elongase among T. grandis elongase genes. 2. TgELO1 expression increased during seed maturation in parallel with accumulation of its putative product cis-11,14-eicosadienoic acid. 3. TgELO1 localized to the endoplasmic reticulum. 4. Co-expression of TgELO1 with TgDES1 in Arabidopsis seeds enabled production of sciadonic acid, demonstrating its essential role in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly, transcriptome sequencing, methylome sequencing under BioProject PRJNA938254 and CNSA CNP0003453
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for metabolic engineering of sciadonic acid production; should be stacked with TgDES1 because TgELO1 alone provides precursor but not final product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close homologs are present in early land plants and algae but absent from flowering plants, supporting pathway loss in angiosperms.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome b5-like heme/steroid binding domain (PF00173) and fatty acid desaturase domain (PF00487); contains three histidine-rich boxes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Torreya grandis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sciadonic acid biosynthesis; fatty acid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ5 double bond into the C20 precursor 20:2Δ11,14 to produce sciadonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant; essential catalytic step for target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Positive effects on human health, including reducing inflammation, lowering triglycerides, preventing blood clots and regulating lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly catalyzes sciadonic acid formation and promotes its accumulation; catalytic specificity depends on histidine-rich boxes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed kernels and increases during seed maturation together with sciadonic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In T. grandis seeds, expression increases during maturation; in Arabidopsis and N. benthamiana, ectopic expression via 35S constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling during seed maturation; metabolite profiling by gas chromatography; subcellular colocalization with ER marker in N. benthamiana; heterologous co-overexpression with TgELO1 in Arabidopsis followed by GC detection of sciadonic acid; motif-swapping transient expression assays in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgDES1 was the only T. grandis desaturase highly similar to the known Anemone Δ5-desaturase and was strongly expressed in seed kernels. 2. TgDES1 expression rose during seed maturation in parallel with sciadonic acid accumulation. 3. TgDES1 localized to the endoplasmic reticulum. 4. Co-expression of TgDES1 with TgELO1 in Arabidopsis seeds generated sciadonic acid de novo. 5. Two histidine-rich boxes of TgDES1 were sufficient to confer sciadonic-acid-producing activity when introduced into Arabidopsis AtSLD2, showing that these motifs are crucial for catalytic specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly, transcriptome sequencing, methylome sequencing under BioProject PRJNA938254 and CNSA CNP0003453
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering sciadonic acid production in crops or heterologous systems; should be combined with TgELO1; histidine-box engineering may transfer Δ5-desaturase activity to other desaturases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TgDES1 belongs to a non-angiosperm-specific desaturase clade likely lost in flowering plants. The first two histidine-rich boxes are crucial determinants of substrate specificity and explain evolutionary gain/loss of sciadonic acid biosynthesis.
